paragraphs.text: Short answer: I found the Zn2+–L‑histidine system in SRD‑46, but I could not isolate the circa‑2005 citation (or extract its specific constants) within the time limit. I did confirm that SRD‑46 contains a substantial Zn–histidine dataset (29 values across six species at near‑ambient conditions). With one broader citation query (or an author/journal hint), I can pinpoint the 2004–2006 paper and list the exact constants it reported.

paragraphs.text:
What I verified in SRD‑46 for Zn2+–L‑histidine
- Entities: metal_208 (Zn2+) and ligand_5898 (L‑histidine; “L‑2‑Amino‑3‑(4‑imidazolyl)propanoic acid”).
- Coverage: 29 equilibrium measurements mapped to six Zn–histidine species definitions.
- Experimental maps: mostly room‑temperature (about 20–30 °C; one map 31–41 °C) and low ionic strength (roughly −0.05 to 0.25 M; plus a few higher‑I maps at ~0.35–0.65, 0.85–1.15, and 2.85–3.15 M).

claims.text: What I verified in SRD‑46 for Zn2+–L‑histidine
mapped to six Zn–histidine species definitions.

paragraphs.text:
Why the exact circa‑2005 paper and its constants aren’t listed yet
- A tight text filter for “2005” within the Zn–histidine VLM set returned no matches in the time available. This often happens if the metadata doesn’t include the year string in the citation field, or the target paper is 2004/2006, or the title lacks “histidine” despite being Zn–histidine work.
